title: Bare ring fails on the Pythagorean identity because it ignores the right-angle hypothesis
informal_statement: The squared-distance Pythagorean goal `(x1-x3)^2+(y1-y3)^2 = (x1-x2)^2+(y1-y2)^2 + (x2-x3)^2+(y2-y3)^2`, which holds only given the right-angle hypothesis `h : (x1-x2)*(x3-x2)+(y1-y2)*(y3-y2) = 0`, attacked with bare `ring` (which never consults hypotheses) before `nlinarith [h]` closed the same episode.

failure.input_context_summary: Goal `(x1-x3)^2+(y1-y3)^2 = (x1-x2)^2+(y1-y2)^2 + (x2-x3)^2+(y2-y3)^2` over ℝ with hypothesis `h : (x1-x2)*(x3-x2)+(y1-y2)*(y3-y2) = 0` in context but unused.
failure.attempt_summary: Submitted `intro x1 y1 x2 y2 x3 y3 h; ring` as the first solve action in a tracked episode. `ring` only closes goals that are polynomial identities true for ALL values of the variables — it never inspects or uses hypotheses. Since this Pythagorean identity is only true given `h` (the right-angle condition), `ring` left an `unsolved_goals` residual: the two sides differ by exactly `-2 * ((x1-x2)*(x3-x2) + (y1-y2)*(y3-y2))`, which is zero only because of `h`. The very next step in the same episode, `nlinarith [h]`, closed it immediately by using the hypothesis directly.
failure.lesson: `ring` proves unconditional polynomial identities only; a goal that depends on a hypothesis (even a purely algebraic, linearly-usable one like a dot-product-zero condition) needs a tactic that consults context, such as `nlinarith [h]`, `linarith`, or `linear_combination` with an explicit coefficient on `h`. Treat `ring` failing on a geometry identity as a signal to check whether an unused hypothesis is load-bearing, not as evidence the identity is false.

notes: Captured via tracked proof-search episode 1ee45ae1-bde1-4cfa-bd4a-68261f9f8fb1 (problem_version e220c871-1bc0-4699-bdf0-f7cd2b8b145a, environment_hash 9e26d28efe88484c36562da27aa22a2cc73a0638d11532cbbc9071a60609025d): step 1 (bare `ring`) recorded kernel_fail with an `unsolved_goals` diagnostic; step 2 (`nlinarith [h]`) closed the same episode kernel_verified as packets/elementary/geometry/pythagorean_right_angle.v1.json.
